prompt: There is a bug in this codebase. When a route template defines a local bean and the template is instantiated with a parameter whose VALUE happens to be the same string as that local bean's name, creating the route fails: references to the local bean from within the template (e.g. {{beanName}}) can no longer be resolved, and the route reports the bean as not found. Expected behavior: a template's local bean should remain usable regardless of whether some parameter value coincides with its name. Find the root cause and fix it.

There is a bug in this codebase's simple expression language parser. When the right-hand side of a logical operator (&& or ||) in a predicate is not a valid operand, the parser's error message names the wrong token: it reports the LEFT-hand side's token instead of the offending right-hand side one. Additionally, operand validation for logical operators is too permissive — nodes that are not actual predicate expressions (binary or logical expressions) are accepted on either side instead of being rejected, which is why the faulty error path is rarely reached. Expected behavior: invalid operands on either side of a logical operator are rejected, and the error message names the token of the side that is actually invalid. Fix both.

There is a bug in this codebase's Vert.x-based platform HTTP component. When a route produces a Server-Sent Events response (content type text/event-stream) from a streaming source, individual events are not delivered to the client as they are produced: the async stream that feeds the response greedily fills its internal buffer before emitting anything, so an event only reaches the client when later data pushes it out. There is also a related defect where a partially filled buffer can be emitted containing trailing bytes beyond what was actually read. Expected behavior: for SSE responses each chunk read is flushed to the client immediately, and emitted buffers contain only the bytes actually read. Fix the streaming behavior.

There is a bug in this codebase's langchain4j AI agent component. Converting a message body that is a wrapped file coming from a remote file system (for example a file consumed over FTP or SFTP, where the wrapped file's underlying file object is NOT a java.io.File) fails with an error stating the wrapped file must contain a java.io.File instance. Expected behavior: such remote wrapped-file bodies convert successfully to the agent body type — the converter should be able to determine the file's name and obtain its bytes without requiring a local java.io.File, and detect the MIME type from the resolved name. Fix the converter.
